upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The draft adds explicit J=1 and J=2 boundary cases, but the promised flow/TU computation is not yet exhibited as a full lifted network with score constraints and dual potentials.

published_prior_art.cite: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds: sharp manipulated-RD bounds in one-cutoff model
gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds and rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation: manipulated-RD bounds without a verified shared nested-window laminar object
bertanha_2020_many_thresholds covers many-threshold RD identification/estimation, not manipulation-driven laminar flow certification
chernozhukov_lee_rosen_2013_intersection_bounds provides generic intersection-bound inference, not the RD dual certificate or joint-vs-separate witness

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The Rosenman et al. entry uses the old title 'Optimized partial identification bounds...' while the current arXiv:1910.02170 record is 'Donor's Deferral and Return Behavior...'; stabilize the bib entry/version.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 / Related work
novelty_flags.one_line: The Rosenman comparison claims sequential convex programming, but the current arXiv record only verifies a general partial-ID procedure for manipulated RD and no location anchor supports that specific algorithmic contrast.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The GRR/Rosenman gap is cited by bibkey only; a flagship shared-laminar-object claim needs a section, equation, page, or quoted statement showing their optimization is windowwise.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises a min-cost-flow / TU certificate, but Exhibit 9.2 only gives an incidence submatrix and feasible point, not the lifted network, objective/slack construction, or TU check including orthant score constraints.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises dual potentials, but §9 does not compute the advertised alpha/beta/gamma potentials or verify complementary slackness on a worked instance.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade_delta
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation rubric only partially delivered: delta_summary promises a quantitative flow/TU certificate, but the draft currently supplies boundary examples without the full computable certificate, leaving tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: §6 already defines Θ_I as {theta(n): n in N(P,rho,s)} and Ψ as the fixed-lambda feasibility system, so the conjecture's equality/orthant iff is mostly a definition unfold plus positive-denominator substitution.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The score inequalities contain arbitrary y_b and lambda_j coefficients; the draft does not specify how these become a pure network-flow/TU constraint matrix, and Assumption 7's 'embeds' clause risks assuming the conclusion.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The strict support-function gap is not quantified over a sign-restricted or named weight vector; Exhibit 9.3 proves only the omega=(1,1) case.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Conjecture 2 with an explicit lifted network: list nodes/arcs, capacities, costs/slacks, matrix blocks, dimensions, and a TU argument that handles or cleanly separates the outcome-weighted orthant inequalities.
Add a §9 exhibit computing the full min-cost-flow certificate and the Conjecture 3 dual potentials on the J=2 witness, including complementary-slackness and a non-normalization check.
Repair Conjecture 1 by stating the causal sharpness lemma not already built into the definition of Θ_I, or demote it to a definition/proposition and make Conjecture 2 the flagship kernel.
Stabilize the Rosenman citation against a specific arXiv version and add location anchors for GRR/Rosenman showing exactly where the one-window or non-laminar computation stops.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: make the flow certificate fully explicit and exhibited, fix the Rosenman/anchor issues, and restate the support-function gap for a named positive weight vector.
